expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: shipment checkpoint distinguishes hyperlink styling, contextual navigation behavior, and fallback rendering safety
expectations.evidence: The output states it cannot verify or demonstrate case-focus coverage due to missing Phase 5b artifacts: “Because Phase 5b artifacts are absent, there is no evidence that the shipment checkpoint process: audited or enforced coverage for hyperlink styling… validated contextual navigation behavior… ensured fallback rendering safety.” This is the opposite of “explicitly covered.”

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase5b
expectations.evidence: The result.md is explicitly framed as Phase 5b checkpoint enforcement evaluation and uses Phase 5b contract language/artifacts: “Primary phase under test: Phase 5b (shipment checkpoint enforcement)” and it checks Phase 5b required outputs (checkpoint_audit, checkpoint_delta, qa_plan_phase5b) and fails specifically for absence of those Phase 5b deliverables.
